_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of basic software applications (e.g., data storage and backup, database applications) and their vulnerabilities.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI application security risks, including prompt injection, jailbreaking, data exfiltration via LLM outputs, and model supply chain vulnerabilities.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-012 (DA, cosine: 0.559), SP-K-012 (SP, cosine: 0.556).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.1/10 (raw 0.508). Shared vocabulary: [data, knowledge, vulnerabilities]. DCWF-only terms [applications, backup, basic, database, software, storage] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, application, chain, exfiltration, injection, jailbreaking] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.877 (P=0.893, R=0.861). P exceeds R by 0.032 — the DCWF element's 15 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:0.0%, N:52.7%, C:47.3%). No dominant classification for "Knowledge of basic software applications (e.g., data storage and backup, databas" vs "Knowledge of AI application security risks, including prompt injection, jailbrea" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.569 > STS 0.508 (gap +0.061). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [data, knowledge, vulnerabilities] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.2%. Shared tokens: [data, knowledge, vulnerabilities]. DCWF-only: [applications, backup, basic, database, software, storage]. WIDAI-only: [ai, application, chain, exfiltration, injection, jailbreaking].
